extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ won the 16.9km Geneva ITT by 13 seconds over CAMPENAERTS to defend overall lead, extending his advantage to 49 seconds on COSTA.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ beat world hour record holder CAMPENAERTS by 13 seconds in 16.9km, signaling Giro ITT threat.
GROßSCHARTNER gained 10.5 seconds on THOMAS at intermediate check, revealing form superior to TT reputation.
CAMPENAERTS crashed first corner of prologue but rebounded to take stage lead, showing recovery resilience patterns.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Dominated stage race with two road wins plus ITT, beating specialist CAMPENAERTS reveals ITT depth beyond climbing.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Made podium by fractions despite losing time to GROßSCHARTNER midway, confirms return to form after inconsistent spring.
rider_signals.CAMPENAERTS Victor: positive: Recovered from prologue crash to lead stage temporarily, demonstrates mental resilience under pressure despite losing to ROGLIČ.
rider_signals.GROßSCHARTNER Felix: positive: Faster than THOMAS at intermediate and finished 31.7 down on CAMPENAERTS, exceeded expectations for non-specialist.
rider_signals.COSTA Rui: positive: Finished 49 seconds down overall in second, strong performance but lacked response to ROGLIČ's TT capability.
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: neutral: Mentioned as former pursuit champion comparing to CAMPENAERTS, no decisive performance data provided.
rider_signals.BEVIN Patrick: neutral: Close to unnamed benchmark at finish but no podium, suggesting solid but not exceptional TT execution.
